channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI experts tamp-down hype, project challenges for 2025
item.description: A trio of AI researchers and experts in Lithuania and England have tossed some cold water on the hype surrounding generative AI and predict that in 2025, companies and investors will have to lower their expectations regarding what the technology and large language models (LLMs) can do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Unauthorized drone flights double; 3 arrested during LA fires
item.description: The FAA instituted temporary flight restrictions in the area. Aircraft are used to drop water on the blazes.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Rising cost of breaches forces organizations to rethink cybersecurity
item.description: Cybersecurity breaches can result in significant financial losses for organizations. Ashish Khanna discusses the rising cost of cybersecurity breaches and how to mitigate them.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple’s packed 2025 iPhone and iPad roadmap just got tipped
item.description: Release windows for everything from the iPhone SE 4 to the iPhone 17 Air have been tipped by reliable reporter Mark Gurman.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Open source trends for 2025 and beyond
item.description: Over the past decades, open source software (OSS) has transformed from being merely a cheaper option into the superior choice for enterprise infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Glovo apuesta por una experiencia fluida y eficiente para el usuario gracias a la tecnología
item.description: Hace tan solo una década, el joven barcelonés Óscar Pierre, recién graduado en Ingeniería Aeroespacial en el Instituto de Tecnología de Georgia, desarrolló la versión inicial de una aplicación de reparto de comida a domicilio bajo una premisa muy clara: “Si puede hacerlo otro por mí, ¿por qué voy a
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Goodbye, copper! NBN Co to upgrade final fixed-line homes to full-fat fibre
item.description: An AU$3 billion equity investment from the Federal Government puts the NBN on track to finish its upgrade plan.
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: Steve Bannon says he will 'take down' the 'truly evil' Elon Musk
item.description: President-elect Trump’s former adviser Steve Bannon has vowed to “take down” tech billionaire Elon Musk, calling him “truly evil.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: TikTok app a ‘national security risk,' Sen. Mark Kelly says
item.description: “I imagine [there's gonna] be a disruption in the service here, you know, starting on the 19th,” Kelly told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Kelly says he is aware TikTok ban 'is controversial,' but there is 'national security risk to' app
item.description: Sen. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) said Sunday he is aware that the possible TikTok ban “is controversial,” yet the app “has a national security risk to it.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: This free tool transforms Chrome into an on-demand scratchpad
item.description: I’ve tried a lot of fancy note-taking apps, but I find myself constantly coming back to the classic old Windows Notepad app on my PC. Sometimes, I even just jot things down on paper. As someone who’s constantly juggling a jillion different things, there’s always a lot to keep track of.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: CES was a giant exercise in AI gaslighting
item.description: For a reporter covering CES, the booth for Samsung Display can seem like a pure distillation of what the tech trade show is all about.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: This video company is positioning itself for a world without TikTok
item.description: As TikTok pleads with the U.S. Supreme Court to let it continue operating in the United States, one video company is taking advantage of the uncertainty around TikTok’s sister app.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Matt Mullenweg: WordPress developer hours cutback may or may not slow innovation
item.description: Automattic CEO Matt Mullenweg said his decision to reduce his team’s weekly hours working on WordPress by 99% , from 4,000 hours to 45 hours, was designed to pressure WP Engine to drop its lawsuit against Mullenweg and Automattic “They don’t actually make WordPress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Zuckerberg says Biden officials would 'scream,' 'curse' at Meta team over COVID takedown requests
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said Biden administration officials would “scream” and “curse” at his employees when they disagreed with the government’s takedown requests over pandemic-related content.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Tech unemployment in the US drops to lowest level in more than two years
item.description: Tech hiring rose in December, dropping the IT unemployment rate to 2% — its lowest since November 2023, according to an analysis of the latest jobs data published today by the US Bureau of Labor statistics (BLS). The overall national unemployment rate held steady at 4.1%, according to the BLS.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chinese auto giant wants to make flying cars your next commute option
item.description: An automotive maker in China has developed a flying car that is setting the stage for a new era in how we think about commuting and connectivity in cities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cohere goes ‘North’ with agentic AI
item.description: Large language model (LLM) provider Cohere has unveiled its new agentic AI offering — North — a low-code platform that will allow enterprises to build and deploy agents across different business functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What to know as TikTok argues in front of Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok is hoping to invalidate a law that would force it to divest from its Chinese parent company or face a ban.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers to seek GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use
item.description: TSA has expanded its use of facial recognition technology to more than 80 airports across the country in recent years. The post Lawmakers to seek GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025
item.description: AI in action: How agencies are building out trusted artificial intelligence capabilities The post Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia launches blueprints to help jump start AI projects
item.description: Give Nvidia credit, it’s not just talking up big ideas, it’s helping its customers achieve them. The vendor recently issued designs for AI factories after hyping up the idea for several months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top tech stealing the show at CES 2025
item.description: Big tech coming in 2025 includes solar umbrellas, AI TVs, smart earbuds and crazy robots. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his take on the wonders revealed at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gleam 1.7 brings faster record updates
item.description: Gleam 1.7 has arrived with faster record updates and a dynamic coder for a custom type. Overall, the latest version of Gleam touts performance and package publishing improvements. This latest update to Gleam was announced January 5 and is accessible from GitHub.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FBI's new warning about AI-driven scams that are after your cash
item.description: FBI warns that criminals are using generative AI to exploit individuals with deceptive tactics. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains their tactics and how to protect yourself from them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
